The Engineering Office establish the status of all projects with unpaid billings, and when found 100 percent completed, submit to the Accounting Division the corresponding Certificate of Completion as basis of the latter in recording the appropriate PPE account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to implement the projects in the first semester of CY 2024 and if found to be incapable of implementing such, to return the funds to the source agency in the immediately succeeding semester.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the CDRRMO prepare annually a comprehensive LDRRMFIP that includes, under separate caption, list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of previous years’ LDRRMF and properly caption it in the AIP.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the full coordination of the Local Finance Committee, Engineering and CDRRM Offices to:
Address and resolve the issues/problems contributing to the delayed implementation and billing procedures of projects;
Review the internal policy on requiring COA’s Final Inspection Result as a requirement for final billing, as it has affected the rate of disbursement of the City; and
Review the unimplemented appropriations for preparedness and mitigation equipment, and consider reprogramming the same to priority PPAs of the ensuing budget year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: (i) train and assign additional personnel in the Centralized MRF to maximize utilization of the equipment; (ii) fast-track the preparation of turnover documents; and (iii) establish coordination with the recipient barangays to ensure that the equipment is utilized adequately upon turnover.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Revisit the Agency’s Internal Control policy and ensure the efficient and timely utilization of funds;
Fast-track the implementation of on-going priority projects and immediately commence the unimplemented projects; and
Include in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) development projects that could really be implemented during the year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to immediately execute the planned restructuring and reorganization of personnel as soon as the IRR of R.A. No. 11964 is released, and provide the audit team with a copy of the approved plantilla and manning complement as soon as they are approved.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:

The City Accountant and the City Treasurer reconcile the discrepancies between the reported balances of RPT and SET Receivable accounts and the Real Property Tax Delinquencies; and

The City accountant prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries, if necessary, to ensure that the reported balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts are accurate and reliable.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Management:
Direct the Accountant and the Property Officer to continuously exert effort in reconciling the variance on their respective records;
Make a representation from the Sangguniang Bayan to obtain an authority to formally transfer the properties, School Buildings, to the respective users; and,
Direct the City Accountant to provide the necessary disclosure required by the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015. Henceforth, adherence to the reporting guidelines should be faithfully complied.

We recommended that Management impose a timeline for the disposal of the City Government’s unserviceable properties to ensure that the said undertaking be accomplished within the year. Further, direct the General Service Office to complete the IIRUP and submit the same to the Appraisal Committee to determine the appraised value of the said properties and to the Accounting Office as basis for reclassification of unserviceable idle properties to other assets.

We recommended that the City Accountant conduct reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that the Management reasonably and reliably determine the reasonableness of every item in the Program of Works (POW) and direct the concerned officials and employees to submit a written explanation/justification on the excess noted; otherwise, cause the refund of the excess amount of ₱13,918,586.17.

We recommended that the Management require the submission of lacking documents necessary for the conduct of technical review and inspection of these projects so that any deficiencies and/or defects could be communicated immediately for timely correction and rectification.
We recommended that the Management review the City Treasurer and Cashier's accountability and ensure that they are adequately bonded by increasing their fidelity bond.

We recommended that the Management adopt a system of payroll payment through the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) and refrain from granting huge amounts of cash advances to eliminate the risk of loss and misappropriation of funds.

We recommended that the Management:
Prepare and design an organizational structure by either merging or expanding the offices to ensure that the staffing complement is aligned with the functions, performance targets, and programs of the City;
Evaluate qualifications of casual employees and job order personnel to fill up the vacancies; and,
Consider hiring professionals or qualified graduates, particularly the beneficiaries of the Iskolar ng Bayugan Program to join the regular workforce of the City.

We recommended for the Management to:
Develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement the projects and priorities that should be accomplished for its timely use without sacrificing the quality of work;
Assigned personnel to monitor prior years’ unimplemented projects and include the status on the report; and
Fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames indicated in the AIP to achieve the desired outcomes.

We recommended that the Management establish a Material Testing Laboratory under the City Engineering Office to expedite material testing of the infrastructure projects and help generate additional income for the City.

We recommended that the Management maximize the utilization of LDRRMF for disaster preparedness, prevention, and mitigation and abide by the rules stated in DILG DMB Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013. Also, to properly plan and include only those priority projects and activities that are deemed necessary, which will support the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management System.
We recommended that the Management observe the provisions of COA Memorandum 2014 – 009 and COA Circular 2012 – 002 to be able to reflect the correct amount of Trust Liability – DRRM Account.

We recommended that the Management revert back to the General Fund the unexpended balance of LDRRMF – MOOE for CY 2012 to 2013 to be able to make the same amount available for other social services after subsequent enactment of the authorities in charge.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the Management direct the City Health Officer to use the DPRI as the ceiling price in the determination of ABC in the procurement of drugs and medicines to comply with the DOH Administrative Order No. 2019-0040 to achieve the most advantageous price.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that Management direct the CAO and the GSO to conduct reconciliation of their respective records and verify/trace the unreconciled balances and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to come up with the correct balances of the accounts.
